title: Newnham Hops Meeting & Pangbourne (Meadow Farm) with Charles

main_route.excerpt: We set off at 7.35. for a hop business meeting with William, Ed [?Davis/Jones] & others at Newnham. Simon had a good meeting.
We drove on to Pangbourne. Meadow Farm, Pangbourne, campsite. It is a beautiful, big, flat [?site] near the river. We arrived at 3.40.
We left across the toll Bridge 60p. The day before we had crossed it twice, overshooting the campsite entrance. M40. Then home.

stops.excerpt: At 11.45. we walked along the meadows into Pangbourne. Then we came back to the van, had lunch & left.

unresolved.issue: Attendee at Newnham hop meeting; surname uncertain from transcription — could be Davis or Jones; not a place
unresolved.excerpt: a hop business meeting with William, Ed [?Davis/Jones] & others at Newnham

boundary_flags: mixed_start: page B_0355_R begins with Sunday (end of previous trip) before Friday August 20th entry
summary: A two-day August 2021 trip with Simon, Eve, and the dog Tinder, stopping at Newnham-on-Severn for a hop industry business meeting before driving on to Meadow Farm campsite at Pangbourne in Berkshire, where Charles joined them from Reading. After a Saturday morning walk into Pangbourne they returned home to Birmingham via the toll bridge and M40.
extraction_notes: The date bundle incorrectly records the ISO year as 2010; 'Friday August 20th' falls on a Friday in 2021, confirming the trip_id year. Page B_0355_R is a mixed page: the opening 'Sunday' paragraph belongs to the previous trip (mixed_start=true). Charles is named as joining from Reading; William is mentioned as attending the Newnham hop meeting but it is unclear whether he travelled with Simon and Eve or arrived independently. Newnham is interpreted as Newnham-on-Severn, Gloucestershire, consistent with the hop-growing area near the Forest of Dean. The toll bridge is Whitchurch Bridge (Pangbourne side, Berkshire/Oxfordshire border).
